post.content:
Hi All,

I am starting  my job hunt and need some help.

* Total years of experience- 2 years 9 Months
* Role - Data Engineer (I am good at this role, I work with Airflow, Kafka, Snowflake, Dbt and AWS as primary tech)
* College - Tier 3
* Preparation time - 3- 6 Months
* Experience with DSA and system design-  Starting to prepare for it. 

I would appreciate if you guys can point me the correct way as I am targetting some top companies. Consider JPMC , Uber etc.
Preparation strategies if any. 
How should I reach out to recruiters, referrals etc. 


I can assure u is 100% committment. 

Thanks for your time and consideration. 

Would appreciate your suggestions.

"System Design Interview – An Insider’s Guide" by Alex Xu is an excellent book. I used it extensively during my preparation.

For system design, I highly recommend Gaurav Sen's content. Additionally, watching mock interviews for low-level design (LLD) helped me understand the process better. The videos where they "teach" tend to be well-structured, but they don’t simulate the real interview experience where the interviewer may constantly change requirements.
There are some very common design patterns that you must know for LLDs. And they are very common intsruments that are used in HLD like CDN and LB you should know them too.

For DSA, I suggest starting with the classic Striver's playlist and the Neetcode roadmap. Once you’re comfortable with them, begin practicing problems randomly to build versatility.
